messages.content: You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como criar um cluster OKE no OCI pela primeira vez?

Para criar um cluster OKE (Oracle Kubernetes Engine) no OCI:

**Pre-requisitos:**
- VCN com subnets (publica e privada)
- Internet Gateway ou NAT Gateway
- Security Lists com regras para Kubernetes
- Compartment com quota para OKE
- IAM policy para gerenciar recursos OKE

**Via Console (Enhanced Cluster - recomendado):**

1. Acesse Developer Services -> Container Clusters (OKE)
2. Selecione o compartment
3. Clique em "Create Cluster"
4. Escolha o workflow:
   - "Quick Create": cria VCN, subnets e recursos automaticamente
   - "Custom Create": usa recursos de rede existentes
5. Configure o cluster:
   - Name: nome do cluster (ex: "oke-producao")
   - Kubernetes Version: versao mais recente estavel [CHECK DOCS]
   - Cluster Type: Enhanced (recomendado) ou Basic
   - Visibility Type: Private (recomendado) ou Public
6. Configure networking:
   - VCN: selecione ou crie nova
   - Subnets para pods, services, load balancers
   - CIDR blocks para pods e services
7. Configure node pools:
   - Node pool name: "node-pool-1"
   - Shape: VM.Standard.E4.Flex (recomendado)
   - Number of nodes: 3 minimo para producao
   - Boot volume size: 50 GB minimo
   - Kubernetes version: mesma do cluster
8. Adicione SSH keys para acesso aos worker nodes
9. Clique em "Create Cluster"

**Via CLI:**
oci ce cluster create \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --name "oke-producao" \
  --kubernetes-version v1.29.1 \
  --vcn-id <vcn-ocid> \
  --endpoint-config '{"isPublicIpEnabled": false, "subnetId": "<subnet-ocid>"}'

**Configurar kubectl:**
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --file $HOME/.kube/config \
  --region <region> \
  --token-version 2.0.0

# Verificar acesso
kubectl get nodes

**[MUTABLE]** Precos de OKE: cluster e gratuito, pague apenas pelos worker nodes.

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingclusterusingcli.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como configurar node pools no OKE e escolher o shape correto?

Node pools no OKE sao grupos de worker nodes com mesma configuracao:

**Criar node pool:**

Via Console:
1. OKE -> clique no cluster -> Node Pools
2. Clique em "Create Node Pool"
3. Configure:
   - Name: nome do node pool
   - Compartment: onde os nodes serao criados
   - Kubernetes version: mesma do cluster ou compativel
4. Configure shape e scaling:
   - Shape: VM.Standard.E4.Flex (recomendado para custo-beneficio)
   - OCPUs: 4-8 para workloads gerais
   - Memory: 16-64 GB conforme necessidade
   - Number of nodes: 3 minimo para HA
   - Enable autoscaling: para scaling automatico
5. Configure boot volume:
   - Size: 50 GB minimo
   - Performance: Balanced ou Higher
6. Configure networking:
   - Subnets: selecione subnets do cluster
   - NSGs: para controle de acesso granular
7. Configure SSH keys
8. Clique em "Create"

**Shapes recomendados:**

| Shape | Use Case | OCPUs | Memoria |
|-------|----------|-------|--------|
| VM.Standard.E4.Flex | Geral | 1-128 | 1-2048 GB |
| VM.Standard.A1.Flex | Custo-beneficio (ARM) | 1-48 | 1-384 GB |
| VM.Optimized3.Flex | Memoria intensiva | 1-128 | 1-2048 GB |
| BM.Standard.E4 | Bare metal | 64 | 1024 GB |

**Node pool autoscaling:**
oci ce node-pool create \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --name "autoscale-pool" \
  --kubernetes-version v1.29.1 \
  --node-shape "VM.Standard.E4.Flex" \
  --node-shape-config '{"ocpus": 4, "memory-in-gbs": 32}' \
  --node-config-details '[{"size": 3}]' \
  --initial-node-labels '{"key": "workload", "value": "general"}'

**Melhores praticas:**
- Use multiplos node pools para diferentes workloads
- Separe workloads criticos com taints e tolerations
- Use autoscaling para workloads variaveis
- Distribua nodes em ADs diferentes para HA
- [MUTABLE] Precos de shapes variam por regiao

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingnodepool.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como fazer deploy de uma aplicacao no OKE usando kubectl?

Deploy de aplicacao no OKE:

**1. Preparar o kubeconfig:**
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --file $HOME/.kube/config \
  --region <region> \
  --token-version 2.0.0

# Verificar conexao
kubectl get nodes
kubectl cluster-info

**2. Criar deployment:**

deployment.yaml:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: minha-api
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: minha-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: minha-api
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: minha-api
        image: iad.ocir.io/<namespace>/minha-api:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "250m"
            memory: "256Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "512Mi"
        env:
        - name: DB_HOST
          value: "mysql-service.default.svc.cluster.local"
        - name: DB_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: db-credentials
              key: password

kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml

**3. Criar service:**

service.yaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: minha-api-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: minha-api
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8080
  type: ClusterIP

kubectl apply -f service.yaml

**4. Expor via Load Balancer:**

lb-service.yaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: minha-api-lb
  annotations:
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-shape: "flexible"
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-shape-flex-min: "10"
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-shape-flex-max: "100"
spec:
  selector:
    app: minha-api
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8080
  type: LoadBalancer

kubectl apply -f lb-service.yaml
kubectl get svc minha-api-lb
# Aguardar EXTERNAL-IP ser atribuido

**5. Verificar deploy:**
kubectl get deployments
kubectl get pods
kubectl get svc
kubectl logs -f deployment/minha-api

**[MUTABLE]** Load Balancer tem custo por hora + bandwidth.

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengdeployingapplications.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como configurar kubectl para acessar o cluster OKE de forma segura?

Configuracao segura de kubectl para OKE:

**1. Gerar kubeconfig:**
# Criar diretorio
mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

# Gerar kubeconfig com token
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --file $HOME/.kube/config \
  --region <region> \
  --token-version 2.0.0

# Verificar
kubectl get nodes

**2. Multiplos clusters:**
# Gerar para cluster especifico
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --file $HOME/.kube/config-oke-prod \
  --region <region>

# Usar kubeconfig especifico
export KUBECONFIG=$HOME/.kube/config-oke-prod
kubectl get nodes

# Ou usar flag
kubectl --kubeconfig $HOME/.kube/config-oke-prod get nodes

**3. Token de autenticacao:**
- Tokens expiram em 24 horas por padrao
- Renove o token:
oci ce cluster generate-token \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --region <region>

**4. Configurar contexto:**
kubectl config use-context context-<cluster-ocid>
kubectl config get-contexts

**5. RBAC - Configurar acesso por usuario:**

# ClusterRole para desenvolvedor
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: developer-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ["", "apps", "batch"]
  resources: ["pods", "deployments", "services", "jobs"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["logs", "exec"]
  verbs: ["get"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: developer-binding
subjects:
- kind: User
  name: "ocid1.user.oc1..<user-ocid>"
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: developer-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

**6. Boas praticas de seguranca:**
- Nunca commite kubeconfig em repositorios
- Use tokens de curta duracao
- Configure RBAC para limitar acesso
- Use namespaces para isolamento
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique versao do token suportada

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengdownloadkubeconfigfile.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como integrar OKE com OCI Load Balancer para expor aplicacoes?

Integracao OKE com OCI Load Balancer:

**1. Load Balancer automatico (via Service):**

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: minha-api
  annotations:
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-shape: "flexible"
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-shape-flex-min: "10"
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-shape-flex-max: "100"
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-security-list-management-mode: "All"
    service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-internal: "false"
spec:
  selector:
    app: minha-api
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8080
  type: LoadBalancer

**2. Annotations disponiveis:**

| Annotation | Descricao |
|-----------|----------|
| oci-load-balancer-shape | flexible, 10Mbps, 100Mbps, 400Mbps, 8000Mbps |
| oci-load-balancer-shape-flex-min | Banda minima (Mbps) |
| oci-load-balancer-shape-flex-max | Banda maxima (Mbps) |
| oci-load-balancer-internal | true para LB interno |
| oci-load-balancer-security-list-management-mode | All, Frontend, None |
| oci-load-balancer-connection-idle-timeout | Timeout em segundos |

**3. Load Balancer interno:**
annotations:
  service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-internal: "true"

**4. Ingress Controller (recomendado para multiplas apps):**

# Instalar NGINX Ingress Controller
helm repo add ingress-nginx https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx
helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx \
  --namespace ingress-nginx \
  --create-namespace \
  --set controller.service.annotations."service\.beta\.kubernetes\.io/oci-load-balancer-shape"=flexible

Ingress resource:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: minha-api-ingress
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  rules:
  - host: api.exemplo.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: minha-api
            port:
              number: 80

**5. SSL/TLS no Load Balancer:**
annotations:
  service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-ssl-ports: "443"
  service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-tls-secret: "tls-secret"

**[MUTABLE]** Load Balancer custo: ~$0.015/hora + bandwidth.

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingloadbalancer.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como configurar autoscaling de pods no OKE com HPA?

Autoscaling de pods no OKE com Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA):

**1. Pre-requisitos - Metrics Server:**
# Verificar se metrics-server esta rodando
kubectl get deployment metrics-server -n kube-system

# Se nao estiver, instale:
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml

**2. Criar HPA:**

apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: minha-api-hpa
  namespace: default
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: minha-api
  minReplicas: 3
  maxReplicas: 20
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: memory
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 80

kubectl apply -f hpa.yaml

**3. HPA via CLI:**
kubectl autoscale deployment minha-api \
  --cpu-percent=70 \
  --min=3 \
  --max=20

**4. Verificar status:**
kubectl get hpa
kubectl describe hpa minha-api-hpa

# Output esperado:
# NAME            REFERENCE              TARGETS   MINPODS   MAXPODS   REPLICAS
# minha-api-hpa   Deployment/minha-api   45%/70%   3         20        5

**5. Custom metrics (OCI Monitoring):**

Para escalar baseado em metricas customizadas:
# Instalar OCI Metrics Adapter
helm install oci-metrics-adapter ./oci-metrics-adapter \
  --namespace oci-metrics \
  --create-namespace

HPA com custom metric:
metrics:
- type: Pods
  pods:
    metric:
      name: http_requests_per_second
    target:
      type: AverageValue
      averageValue: "1000"

**6. Cluster Autoscaler:**
Para escalar nodes automaticamente:
oci ce node-pool update \
  --node-pool-id <nodepool-ocid> \
  --node-config-details '[{"size": 3, "isAutoscaleEnabled": true}]' \
  --node-autoscaling-config '{"minNodes": 3, "maxNodes": 10}'

**Boas praticas:**
- Sempre configure resource requests/limits nos pods
- Use minReplicas >= 3 para HA
- Teste autoscaling com load testing
- Monitore eventos do HPA: kubectl describe hpa

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengusinghorizontalpodautoscaler.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como fazer upgrade de versao do Kubernetes no OKE sem downtime?

Upgrade de versao do Kubernetes no OKE:

**Estrategia de upgrade:**
- OKE suporta upgrade in-place sem downtime
- Upgrades sao feitos node por node (rolling)
- Pods sao rescheduled automaticamente

**1. Verificar versoes disponiveis:**
oci ce cluster options get \
  --cluster-option-id all \
  --query "data.kubernetes-versions"

**2. Upgrade do control plane:**

Via Console:
1. OKE -> clique no cluster -> Edit
2. Altere Kubernetes Version
3. Salve (control plane e atualizado primeiro)

Via CLI:
oci ce cluster update \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --kubernetes-version v1.30.1

**3. Upgrade dos worker nodes:**

**Metodo A - In-place upgrade (recomendado):**
# Upgrade node pool
oci ce node-pool update \
  --node-pool-id <nodepool-ocid> \
  --kubernetes-version v1.30.1

# OKE faz rolling upgrade automaticamente
# Monitore o progresso:
oci ce node-pool get --node-pool-id <nodepool-ocid>

**Metodo B - Blue/Green (mais seguro):**
# 1. Criar novo node pool com nova versao
oci ce node-pool create \
  --cluster-id <cluster-ocid> \
  --name "node-pool-v130" \
  --kubernetes-version v1.30.1 \
  --node-shape "VM.Standard.E4.Flex"

# 2. Cordone e drene nodes antigos
kubectl cordon <old-node>
kubectl drain <old-node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data

# 3. Verificar se pods migraram
kubectl get pods -o wide

# 4. Deletar node pool antigo
oci ce node-pool delete --node-pool-id <old-nodepool-ocid>

**4. Verificar upgrade:**
kubectl version
kubectl get nodes
# Verificar se todos os nodes estao na nova versao

**5. Pre-upgrade checklist:**
- Backup de configuracoes criticas
- Verificar compatibilidade de manifests com nova versao
- Verificar depreciacoes de API
- Testar em ambiente de staging
- [CHECK DOCS] Verificar breaking changes da versao

**6. Pos-upgrade:**
- Verificar health de todos os pods
- Validar funcionalidade da aplicacao
- Monitorar logs por erros

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengupgradingk8sworkernode.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como resolver problemas de provisioning de nodes no OKE?

Troubleshooting de provisioning de nodes no OKE:

**1. Verificar status do node pool:**
oci ce node-pool get --node-pool-id <nodepool-ocid>

# Status possiveis:
# CREATING, ACTIVE, UPDATING, DELETING, FAILED

**2. Problemas comuns:**

**a) Nodes em estado Failed:**
# Verificar logs do node
oci ce node-pool-node get \
  --node-pool-id <nodepool-ocid> \
  --node-id <node-ocid>

# SSH para o node (se possivel)
ssh -i <ssh-key> opc@<node-ip>
sudo journalctl -u kubelet -f

**b) Quota insuficiente:**
Erro: "Service limit reached"
Solucao: Solicitar aumento de quota no OCI Console

**c) Subnet sem IPs disponiveis:**
Erro: "No available IPs in subnet"
Solucao: Usar subnet com CIDR maior ou criar nova subnet

**d) Security List bloqueando:**
Verifique regras de entrada:
- Porta 22 (SSH)
- Portas 10250-10256 (Kubernetes)
- Porta 12250 (Kubelet)
- ICMP para health checks

**3. Verificar logs do cluster:**
# Via Console: OKE -> Cluster -> Logs
# Via OCI Logging
oci logging search logs \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --search-query "search "oci-oke" | sort by datetime desc"

**4. Diagnosticar com kubectl:**
# Verificar events
kubectl get events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'

# Verificar nodes
kubectl describe node <node-name>

# Verificar pods do sistema
kubectl get pods -n kube-system

**5. Solucoes:**

| Problema | Solucao |
|----------|--------|
| Quota | Solicitar aumento no console |
| IP esgotado | Expandir subnet CIDR |
| DNS | Verificar DNS resolver na VCN |
| Image pull | Verificar OCIR credentials |
| Storage | Verificar provisioner CSI |

**6. Recriar node pool:**
Se tudo falhar:
oci ce node-pool delete --node-pool-id <nodepool-ocid>
oci ce node-pool create ...

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengtroubleshooting.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como monitorar a saude do cluster OKE e configurar alertas?

Monitoramento do cluster OKE:

**1. OCI Monitoring - Metricas nativas:**

Metricas do cluster:
- CPU e memoria dos worker nodes
- Pod count por node
- Node status (Ready/NotReady)
- Volume usage

Metricas do Kubernetes (via OCI Monitoring agent):
- Pod restart count
- Container CPU/memory
- Deployment replica status

**2. Instalar OCI Monitoring Agent:**
# O agent e instalado automaticamente em nodes OKE
# Verificar status:
kubectl get pods -n oci-monitoring

**3. Configurar alarmes:**

# Alarme para node NotReady
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --display-name "OKE-Node-NotReady" \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_computeagent" \
  --query "NodeStatus[1m]{status = "NotReady"}.sum()" \
  --threshold 1 \
  --comparison-operator GREATER_THAN \
  --notification-topic-id <topic-ocid>

# Alarme para pod restart
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --display-name "OKE-Pod-Restart" \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --namespace "oci_containerengine_cluster" \
  --query "PodRestartCount[5m].sum()" \
  --threshold 5 \
  --comparison-operator GREATER_THAN \
  --notification-topic-id <topic-ocid>

**4. OCI Logging:**
- Configure logs do cluster
- Logs de audit para atividades
- Logs de container para aplicacoes

**5. Dashboards recomendados:**

**Cluster Health:**
- Node status (Ready/NotReady)
- Pod status por namespace
- CPU/Memory utilization
- Volume usage

**Application Health:**
- Pod restart count
- Container errors
- Request latency
- Error rate

**6. Prometheus + Grafana:**
# Instalar Prometheus
helm install prometheus prometheus-community/prometheus \
  --namespace monitoring --create-namespace

# Instalar Grafana
helm install grafana grafana/grafana \
  --namespace monitoring

# Acessar Grafana
kubectl port-forward svc/grafana 3000:80

**7. Alertas criticos:**
- Node NotReady > 5 minutos
- Pod crash loop (> 5 restarts)
- CPU > 85% nos nodes
- Memory > 90% nos nodes
- Pending pods > 10

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengmonitoring.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide technical guidance on Kubernetes cluster creation, node pools, and deployments.
Como configurar networking avancado no OKE com Flannel e Cilium?

Networking avancado no OKE:

**1. CNI Plugins suportados:**

**Flannel (padrao):**
- Overlay network com VXLAN
- Simples e estavel
- Ideal para workloads basicos
- Configurado automaticamente no OKE

**Cilium (avancado):**
- eBPF-based networking
- Network policies avancadas
- Observabilidade integrada
- Melhor performance

**2. Configurar Cilium no OKE:**

# Instalar Cilium via Helm
helm repo add cilium https://helm.cilium.io/
helm install cilium cilium/cilium \
  --namespace kube-system \
  --set kubeProxyReplacement=strict \
  --set k8sServiceHost=<api-server-endpoint> \
  --set k8sServicePort=6443

**3. Network Policies com Cilium:**

apiVersion: cilium.io/v2
kind: CiliumNetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-frontend-to-backend
spec:
  endpointSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: backend
  ingress:
  - fromEndpoints:
    - matchLabels:
        app: frontend
    toPorts:
    - ports:
      - port: "8080"
        protocol: TCP

**4. Service Mesh com OKE:**

# Instalar Istio
istioctl install --set profile=demo -y

# Habilitar sidecar injection
kubectl label namespace default istio-injection=enabled

**5. DNS no OKE:**
- CoreDNS e configurado automaticamente
- Service discovery: <service>.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local
- Custom DNS: configure CoreDNS ConfigMap

**6. IPv6 no OKE:**
- Suporte limitado [CHECK DOCS]
- Configure VCN com IPv6 CIDR
- Habilite IPv6 no node pool

**7. Troubleshooting de rede:**
# Verificar pods de rede
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep -E "flannel|cilium|coredns"

# Testar conectividade entre pods
kubectl exec -it <pod> -- ping <outro-pod-ip>

# Verificar routes
kubectl exec -it <pod> -- ip route

**Boas praticas:**
- Use network policies para seguranca
- Monitore latency entre pods
- Configure DNS search domains
- [CHECK DOCS] Verifique suporte atual de Cilium no OKE

Referencia: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengnetworkconfig.htm
